growth_records.lvlwhy: species-level trait compilation (Madin) — no strain recorded in source
growth_records.cite: Madin JS et al. A synthesis of bacterial and archaeal phenotypic trait data. Sci Data 7:170 (2020); source dataset: engqvist.
growth_records.notes: Species-level trait record; culture mode/pH often unspecified; medium + uptake/secretion pending literature curation.

growth_records.medium: Hot Lake Heterotrophic (HLH) medium + 5 mM glucose + 1x Wolfe's vitamins

up.method: not quantified as a specific rate; glucose was sole carbon source (5 mM)

sec.method: NMR (Chenomx) of extracellular metabolites at mid-log (20 h, glucose)
NMR at mid-log 20 h; secreted by axenic Halomonas HL-48 on glucose

growth_records.cite: Khan, Nymul et al. Phenotypic responses to interspecies competition and commensalism in a naturally-derived microbial co-culture. Scientific Reports (2018). DOI:10.1038/s41598-017-18630-1. [PMC5762899]
growth_records.method: reported (mu_max from log-linear regression of species-specific growth curve); doubling time calculated td=ln2/mu=0.6931/0.307=2.258 h
growth_records.snippet: The axenic cultures of Halomonas HL-48 exhibited higher maximum specific growth rates (0.307 h-1) on glucose as compared to axenic Marinobacter HL-58 (0.215 h-1).
growth_records.notes: Axenic monoculture of prokaryote Halomonas sp. HL-48 on 5 mM glucose. mu_max=0.307 h-1 reported (Fig.1C slope). td=ln2/mu=0.6931/0.307=2.258 h. Extracellular acetate and ethanol measured by NMR at mid-log (20 h) but absolute concentrations only in Fig.1E, not in text. No mmol/gDW/h uptake/secretion rates reported; qS not derivable (no biomass or delta-substrate values given). pH 8, 30 C, shake flask aerobic.

growth_records.medium: HLH medium + 5 mM glucose (co-culture with Marinobacter HL-58)

up.method: shared glucose (competition); specific rate not quantified

sec.method: NMR mid-log 20 h; acetate decreased vs Halomonas axenic (p=0.02)
NMR mid-log 20 h; ethanol 47.6% lower than axenic Halomonas control (p<0.001)

growth_records.cite: Khan, Nymul et al. Phenotypic responses to interspecies competition and commensalism in a naturally-derived microbial co-culture. Scientific Reports (2018). DOI:10.1038/s41598-017-18630-1. [PMC5762899]
growth_records.method: calculated from reported '6% increase' over axenic: 0.307 x 1.06 = 0.3254 h-1; td=ln2/0.3254=2.130 h
growth_records.snippet: Halomonas HL-48 exhibited only a 6% increase in growth rate while Marinobacter HL-58 ... decreased its specific growth rate by 20%
growth_records.notes: Halomonas HL-48 in competitive co-culture on glucose. mu calculated from stated +6% vs axenic 0.307: 0.307*1.06=0.3254 h-1 (paper gives % change, not absolute co-culture mu). td=ln2/0.3254=2.130 h. Acetate and ethanol both decreased vs axenic (Fig.1E); ethanol -47.6% (p<0.001), acetate p=0.02. Absolute metabolite values only in figure.

growth_records.cite: Waritthorn Thanakarn et al. Reducing the sensitivity of Halomonas sp. to oxygen availability through adaptive laboratory evolution. Microbial Cell Factories (2026). DOI:10.1186/s12934-026-02951-w. [PMC12997722]
growth_records.snippet: The optimal condition was selected as the LB medium supplemented with 8.5% NaCl in total (LB85 medium). For this condition, the specific growth rate of the culture and the maximum cell density measured as optical density were 0.55 +/- 0.10
growth_records.notes: Reported mu=0.55+/-0.10 h-1 (n=6). Doubling time computed td=ln2/mu=0.693/0.55=1.26 h. Max OD600=1.00+/-0.05. No uptake/secretion rates reported for this condition. Micro-aerated (clear film over microwell).

growth_records.cite: Waritthorn Thanakarn et al. Reducing the sensitivity of Halomonas sp. to oxygen availability through adaptive laboratory evolution. Microbial Cell Factories (2026). DOI:10.1186/s12934-026-02951-w. [PMC12997722]
growth_records.snippet: the highest mean specific growth rate (1.32 +/- 0.20 h-1) was achieved in low salt (4.0% NaCl) concentration
growth_records.notes: Reported mu=1.32+/-0.20 h-1 at 4.0% NaCl (highest of the salt screen, n=6). Doubling time td=0.693/1.32=0.525 h. No uptake/secretion rates.

growth_records.cite: Waritthorn Thanakarn et al. Reducing the sensitivity of Halomonas sp. to oxygen availability through adaptive laboratory evolution. Microbial Cell Factories (2026). DOI:10.1186/s12934-026-02951-w. [PMC12997722]
growth_records.snippet: reduced growth rate from 0.43 +/- 0.06 h-1 at 5.0% DO to 0.26 +/- 0.05 h-1 at 1.0% DO
growth_records.notes: Bioreactor batch mu(max)=0.43+/-0.06 h-1 at 5.0% DO (also cited as ca. 0.43 h-1 = critical/max dilution rate; batch mu varied 0.37-0.49). td=0.693/0.43=1.61 h. No uptake/secretion rates.

growth_records.cite: Waritthorn Thanakarn et al. Reducing the sensitivity of Halomonas sp. to oxygen availability through adaptive laboratory evolution. Microbial Cell Factories (2026). DOI:10.1186/s12934-026-02951-w. [PMC12997722]
growth_records.snippet: reduced growth rate from 0.43 +/- 0.06 h-1 at 5.0% DO to 0.26 +/- 0.05 h-1 at 1.0% DO

growth_records.cite: Waritthorn Thanakarn et al. Reducing the sensitivity of Halomonas sp. to oxygen availability through adaptive laboratory evolution. Microbial Cell Factories (2026). DOI:10.1186/s12934-026-02951-w. [PMC12997722]
growth_records.snippet: Growth was severely impeded (0.09 +/- 0.03 h-1) at 0% DO ... decreased dramatically to 0.62 +/- 0.21 OD600

growth_records.strain: DSM7328 ALE-evolved population (185 generations at 0.5% DO)
growth_records.cite: Waritthorn Thanakarn et al. Reducing the sensitivity of Halomonas sp. to oxygen availability through adaptive laboratory evolution. Microbial Cell Factories (2026). DOI:10.1186/s12934-026-02951-w. [PMC12997722]
growth_records.snippet: Halomonas sp. achieved comparable growth at low-to-moderate (0.5%-3.0%) and moderate-to-high (3.0-5.0%) dissolved oxygen availability (OD600 = 4.46 +/- 0.29 & mu = 0.40 h-1 and 4.96 +/- 0.81 & mu = 0.35 h-1, respectively)
growth_records.notes: ALE-evolved population: mu=0.40 h-1, max OD600=4.46+/-0.29 at low-to-moderate DO. td=0.693/0.40=1.73 h. Post-ALE PHB yield +21%, ectoine -79% (intracellular, mg/OD600; not secretion, not in flux units).

growth_records.strain: DSM7328 ALE-evolved population (185 generations at 0.5% DO)
growth_records.cite: Waritthorn Thanakarn et al. Reducing the sensitivity of Halomonas sp. to oxygen availability through adaptive laboratory evolution. Microbial Cell Factories (2026). DOI:10.1186/s12934-026-02951-w. [PMC12997722]
growth_records.notes: ALE-evolved population: mu=0.35 h-1, max OD600=4.96+/-0.81 at moderate-to-high DO. td=0.693/0.35=1.98 h.
